paper_title: Monitoring disease activity of pollen allergies: What crowdsourced data are telling us

methods.description: This method involves using the Google Ads Keyword Planner to collect web search data related to hay fever and pollen allergies across Germany and Sweden from January 2017 to December 2020. The data helps analyze search behavior across different vegetational and climatic zones.

steps.description: The Google Ads Keyword Planner was used to obtain web search data related to hay fever and pollen allergies across Germany and Sweden from January 2017 to December 2020.
steps.searchable_summary: Use Google Ads Keyword Planner to collect search data for hay fever and pollen allergies
inputs: keywords for hay fever and pollen allergies

steps.description: In this study, Google Ads Keyword Planner was used to identify the search terms ('keywords') associated with the German and Swedish words for 'hay fever' and 'pollen allergy'.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify search terms for hay fever and pollen allergy in German and Swedish
inputs: German and Swedish words for hay fever and pollen allergy

steps.description: Based on these 2 words, a list of relevant keywords and their monthly search volume from January 2017 to December 2020 was generated for each of the 16 German federal states and 21 Swedish counties as well as for each country as a whole.
steps.searchable_summary: Generate list of keywords and monthly search volume for regions

steps.description: All keywords were examined whether they explicitly referred to pollen allergies, hay fever, or allergies in general, and then classified accordingly into 'specific' and 'non-specific' keywords.
steps.searchable_summary: Classify keywords into specific and non-specific categories

steps.description: Specific keywords were further assigned to seven inductively created categories.
steps.searchable_summary: Assign specific keywords to predefined categories

methods.description: This method involves statistical procedures to analyze the collected data, including calculating correlation coefficients and determining regional differences in search volume.

steps.description: To describe the relationship between the investigated variables, SPSS version 25.0 (IBM Corp) was used to calculate the non-parametric Spearman correlation coefficient (r).
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate Spearman correlation coefficient using SPSS

steps.description: Kruskal-Wallis one-way analysis of variance was used to determine regional differences in search volume per 100,000 inhabitants (standardized).
steps.searchable_summary: Perform Kruskal-Wallis analysis for regional differences
